title: Not Boring Movies
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: Pareto-optimal database challenge: the single SQL filter plus ORDER BY solution is also the speed extreme and memory extreme. Per the repo standard (no coincident files), only solution.sql is shipped; there is no separate solution-runtime or solution-memory file.

variants.languageReason: The task is a relational selection with a required order over one table. SQL executes the predicates and ordering inside the database engine, avoids client-side materialization, and is the fastest and leanest accepted interface for a LeetCode Database problem.

languageAlternatives.assessment: Not a LeetCode Database submission language for this schema; a client-side solution would need to extract rows before filtering and sorting.
Unsupported for this database prompt, and manual row handling would add transfer and memory overhead compared with pushed-down SQL predicates.
Strong for native in-memory filtering and sorting, but unsupported here and dominated by executing the relational query where the data resides.
Unsupported for this SQL schema submission; application-side slices would allocate more than the database engine's native query path.
Not the accepted query interface for the database track; managed row objects and client-side sorting would be heavier than the SQL plan.

Python/Pandas can express the same filter and sort, but it materializes DataFrame data; the other scripting languages are not the intended database submission path. SQL is leaner and optimizer-driven.

variants.approachJustification: Filter Cinema rows with id % 2 = 1 and description <> 'boring', project the required columns, then order the qualifying rows by rating descending.
variants.timeComplexity: O(C + Q log Q) logical work without a rating index
variants.spaceComplexity: O(Q) output plus optimizer-dependent sort workspace

reasoningSummary: The problem is a direct selection over Cinema plus the required descending rating order. Filtering on odd ids and non-boring descriptions before sorting minimizes the rows fed into the only expensive operator.
timeComplexity: O(C + Q log Q) logical work without a rating index
spaceComplexity: O(Q) output plus optimizer-dependent sort workspace
